mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) indicate stronger household balance sheets, boosting consumption demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tight labor markets that compress wage growth and boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS) rise while industrial production (INDPRO) simultaneously declines, it indicates a supply shock combined with monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and construction-related earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in rising HOUST periods do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) accelerates beyond a 3-month annualized growth rate of 3.0%, it triggers fears of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding business activity and wage-driven consumption, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day forward return distribution shows no significant difference between positive and negative PAYEMS growth months (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) outpaces industrial production (INDPRO) by more than 1.5 percentage points, it signals strong demand absorption of supply, boosting corporate margins and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread signal fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the interaction term's predictive power is fully absorbed by a single-factor market model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, a subsequent decline in the jobless rate below 5.5% signals a labor market inflection point that boosts consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in reversal episodes are not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10), or the signal produces negative returns in > 60% of post-peak instances across multiple recessions.
